## [03:01 - 06:03]

And basically I'm sure you've seen plenty of squeeze pages. That's a web page where someone comes to your web page and they basically have a decision of either leaving their email address to get something from you or also leaving your website. Basically you squeeze them into a decision. You force a decision. They either say yes or they say no. If they say yes, they join your list. If they say no, they leave your site. So like I mean a typical squeeze page might just be a headline, maybe a video, and an opt-in box. Or it could be a headline, a bunch of copy, then an opt-in box. Or it could be just a video and an opt-in box. Basically when they come to that site, they either can opt-in or they can leave. Now opt-in rates can really vary. You could be at 5% of the people that come to your site opt-in. You could be at 50%. One of the things I've done a lot of testing around squeeze pages. And that's one of the most fruitful places where you can test. It's very easy to test. Do like a split test of two designs, two headlines, two videos, two whatever. You test them against each other and you see which one does best. The beautiful thing, when you're doing a test, you need to drive a lot of traffic through the test and you need to get a lot of people taking actions. So if you're selling something, it will take you longer to do a test than if you're simply going testing an opt-in page. Because it's easy to get a lot of people to come to an opt-in page and it's easy to get those actions to test. Now like when you're testing, it all comes down to how many actions you've had. If you're selling an e-book and you're selling three of them a day, it might take you a couple months to have a test. Or maybe six months to have a test and get statistically valid results. ## [12:10 - 14:52]

You know, if they're coming into for a different bribe, then you're going to want to have them, you know, probably going onto different lists. But we'll get to that later. So basically, the squeeze page, what that does is it's got the bribe. Okay. And the bribe is what they're going to get. So basically, you want to create a bribe and the bribe could be any number of things. It could be a PDF report that they get. But basically, you have to figure out what they want and what you can deliver to them. And it doesn't have to be that fancy. It could be a simple PDF report that tells them the four tricks that you learned on how to play that big guitar or how to grow big tomatoes. So basically, the squeeze page has copy in it that tells them about the bribe that they're going to get. So then after they opt in and they're joining your list, then you send them the bribe.
